design_decisions.d0r_pending_approval: D0.R made 2 assumption + 0 definition change(s) PROVISIONALLY (pending approval, direction-of-truth align/correct/discharge): ass:selected-quantile-kernel-measurability, ass:oracle-transform-lambda-regularity
design_decisions.d0_open_oeq_residuals: Open-ended question(s) left as the note's acknowledged open problem for D0.5 review: oeq:boundary-uniform-inference

added_assumptions.label: ass:measurable-maximum-regularity
added_assumptions.statement: underline_pi_d and overline_pi_d are P_X-measurable, and for each sigma the map (x,t) -> N_sigma(x,t) is jointly measurable and continuous in t on [underline_pi_d(x), overline_pi_d(x)]; for every lambda, the argmax correspondences of t -> N_sigma(x,t) - lambda t and t -> N_sigma(x,t) / t over that interval admit P_X-measurable selections.

added_assumptions.label: ass:crossfit-orthogonal-remainder
added_assumptions.statement: For each sigma, on a fixed neighborhood Lambda_sigma of lambda_sigma^*, the corrected cross-fitted transform satisfies sup_{lambda in Lambda_sigma} |hat_Phi_{sigma,n}(lambda)-Phi_sigma(lambda;P)-B_{sigma,lambda,n}(P)-(P_n-P) psi_{sigma,lambda,n}(O;P)| = O_P(r_n^2)+o_P((n h_n)^(-1/2)).

added_assumptions.label: ass:boundary-uniform-transform-process
added_assumptions.statement: Uniformly over P in P_0 and lambda in Lambda_{-1} union Lambda_{+1}, sqrt(n h_n){hat_Phi_{sigma,n}(lambda)-Phi_sigma(lambda;P)-B_{sigma,lambda,n}(P)} admits a tight asymptotically Gaussian process limit G_{sigma,P}(lambda) in C(Lambda_sigma), the joint multiplier process hat_G_{sigma,n}^*(lambda)=sqrt(h_n/n) sum_i xi_i{hat_psi_{sigma,lambda,n}(O_i)-hat_Phi_{sigma,n}(lambda)} consistently estimates the same joint law conditionally on the data, and sup_lambda sqrt(n h_n)|B_{sigma,lambda,n}(P)| -> 0.

added_assumptions.label: ass:localized-score-smoothness
added_assumptions.statement: For each sigma and lambda in a neighborhood of lambda_sigma^*, the corrected residual conditional mean r_{sigma,lambda}(a,x)=E[G_sigma(O,pi_{sigma,lambda}(x);P)-N_sigma(x,pi_{sigma,lambda}(x)) | D=a,X=x] satisfies r_{sigma,lambda}(d,x)=0 and has two bounded derivatives in a uniformly over x and lambda, and along every regular nuisance path through the truth the associated bridge version preserves the identity E[G_{sigma,r}(O,t)-N_{sigma,r}(X,t) | D=d,X=x]=0 to first order at r=0; the corresponding conditional second moments are continuous at a=d and dominated by an integrable envelope.

added_assumptions.label: ass:selected-mass-estimability
added_assumptions.statement: s_d is L2(P_X)-estimable at rate O_P(r_n).

added_assumptions.label: ass:band-estimability
added_assumptions.statement: underline_pi_d and overline_pi_d are L2(P_X)-estimable at rate O_P(r_n).

added_assumptions.label: ass:trimmed-numerator-estimability
added_assumptions.statement: Psi_d^L and Psi_d^U are uniformly L2(P_X)-estimable over t in [underline_pi_d(X), overline_pi_d(X)] at rate O_P(r_n).

added_assumptions.label: ass:local-dose-density
added_assumptions.statement: There exist constants 0 < c_f < C_f < infinity such that c_f <= f_d(X) <= C_f for P_X-almost every X.

added_assumptions.label: ass:local-dose-smoothness
added_assumptions.statement: There exists delta_d > 0 such that, for P_X-almost every X and every u in [underline_pi_d(X) / s_d(X), 1 - overline_pi_d(X) / s_d(X)], the maps a -> s_a(X), a -> f_a(X), and a -> Q_a(u,X) are twice continuously differentiable on [d - delta_d, d + delta_d] intersect supp(D), with second derivatives uniformly bounded in absolute value by a finite constant.

added_assumptions.label: ass:quantile-regularity
added_assumptions.statement: For P_X-almost every x, the conditional law of Y given S=1, D=d, X=x has a density on [y_min,y_max] that is continuous and bounded away from 0 on neighborhoods of Q_d(underline_pi_d(x) / s_d(x), x) and Q_d(1 - overline_pi_d(x) / s_d(x), x).

added_assumptions.label: ass:kernel-regularity
added_assumptions.statement: K is bounded, symmetric, compactly supported, integral K(u) du = 1, integral u K(u) du = 0, and integral u^2 |K(u)| du < infinity.

added_assumptions.label: ass:undersmoothing
added_assumptions.statement: h_n -> 0, n h_n -> infinity, and (n h_n)^(1/2) h_n^2 -> 0.

added_assumptions.label: ass:uniform-transform-consistency
added_assumptions.statement: For each sigma in {-1,+1}, there is a compact Lambda_sigma containing lambda_sigma^*(P) in its interior such that sup_{lambda in Lambda_sigma}|hat_Phi^or_{sigma,n}(lambda)-Phi_sigma(lambda;P)| ->p 0 in the oracle known-nuisance experiment.

added_assumptions.label: ass:selected-quantile-kernel-measurability
added_assumptions.statement: s_d is P_X-measurable and the selected-outcome quantile map (u,x) -> Q_d(u,x) is jointly measurable on [0,1] x X_space.

added_assumptions.label: ass:oracle-transform-lambda-regularity
added_assumptions.statement: For each sigma and compact Lambda_sigma, the oracle localized score class {psi_{sigma,lambda,n}(.;P): lambda in Lambda_sigma} has measurable lambda-continuous versions, a scalar Lipschitz modulus in lambda, envelope |psi_{sigma,lambda,n}(O;P)| <= C{1+|K((D-d)/h_n)|/h_n}, variance sup_{lambda in Lambda_sigma} Var_P(psi_{sigma,lambda,n}(O;P)) <= C/h_n, and a residual conditional mean that is continuous at D=d uniformly in lambda and dominated on some neighborhood of d by an integrable envelope.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp covariate-conditional identified set for the always-takers average dose-response function under a sufficient-set sample-selection restriction with continuous treatment (Lee–Liu 2025, the deferred sharp case): characterize it as the value of a global support-function / linear-fractional program over feasible conditional always-takers proportions π_AT(X), coupled across X by the shared denominator E[π_AT(X)] and solved via a scalar Dinkelbach/KKT threshold; with a primitive-verifiable condition C under which the pointwise-Fréchet plug-in is exactly sharp; plus a doubly-robust kernel-localized estimator of the endpoints and inference over the identified set
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-repo warning is pid_dose_response_shape_v1, whose headline law-level sharpness claim failed because pointwise continuous-dose fibers did not paste into a globally sharp set. The new core does not repeat that mistake; it promotes the missing law-level object itself to the theorem target and makes the shared denominator E[pi(X)] the central coupling primitive. Other nearby banked proposals, such as pid_dose_response_iv_v1 and pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2, use fractional or active-threshold programs in different latent-mixture settings, but none state the Lee-Liu sufficient-set always-takers ADRF support function or its common-threshold collapse frontier.","published_axis":"LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee is the direct comparator and leaves the sufficient-set covariate case unresolved once pi_AT(X) is only Frechet bounded. Semenova2024GeneralizedLee shows how Lee-type identified sets and orthogonal support-function inference look when the always-taker law is identified, but it does not solve a denominator-coupled selector problem. JonzonGabrielSjolanderSachs2025CovariatesBounds clarifies why pointwise conditional sharpness need not aggregate to uniform sharpness, yet does not derive the model-specific global program or an iff collapse condition. BenMichael2025ConditionalLP covers expectations of conditional LP optima, not global linear-fractional bounds coupled through E[pi(X)]. That leaves a precise gap a referee can recognize as real: a sharp law-level representation and its threshold frontier for the continuous-treatment sufficient-set always-takers ADRF remain unpublished."}
seed_list: Global support-function characterization of the sufficient-set always-takers ADRF as a measurable-selector linear-fractional program with a scalar Dinkelbach root.
Pointwise Frechet plug-in is sharp iff a common-threshold KKT certificate aligns the local ratio optimizer with the global denominator-coupled program.
There is a nondegenerate region of laws where pointwise sharp conditional fibers fail to paste into a globally sharp always-taker ADRF bound.
A kernel-localized doubly robust estimator of the Dinkelbach endpoint is asymptotically linear on a strict-margin regular submodel.
Uniform identified-set inference at active-set ties is governed by the directional derivative of the Dinkelbach root map, not an ordinary delta method.
A finite-bin piecewise-affine threshold formula approximates the continuous-dose sufficient-set endpoint and exposes the active constraints explicitly.
Retained assignment memory changes the principal-stratum feasible polytope and yields a distinct linear-fractional always-taker/complier endpoint.
On the strict-margin submodel, the sufficient-set endpoint has a semiparametric efficiency bound distinct from both point-identified ADRF and conditional-LP bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Global support-function characterization of the sufficient-set always-takers ADRF as a measurable-selector linear-fractional program with a scalar Dinkelbach root.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Sharply characterize the covariate-conditional always-takers ADRF identified set under Lee-Liu sufficient-set selection by replacing pointwise Frechet pi_AT(X) plug-ins with a global denominator-coupled support-function program.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from Lee-Liu plus Dinkelbach because the ratio separates pointwise in X.
seed_details.rebuttal: The ratio does not separate because E[pi(X)] is shared across X; the missing theorem is measurable global compatibility, not scalar fractional algebra.

seed_details.one_line: Pointwise Frechet plug-in is sharp iff a common-threshold KKT certificate aligns the local ratio optimizer with the global denominator-coupled program.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Sharply characterize the covariate-conditional always-takers ADRF identified set under Lee-Liu sufficient-set selection by replacing pointwise Frechet pi_AT(X) plug-ins with a global denominator-coupled support-function program.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only a corollary once the global support function is proved.
seed_details.rebuttal: The collapse theorem is the operational frontier result; it names the primitive condition under which local sharpness equals global sharpness, which the anchor literature explicitly lacks.

seed_details.one_line: There is a nondegenerate region of laws where pointwise sharp conditional fibers fail to paste into a globally sharp always-taker ADRF bound.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Sharply characterize the covariate-conditional always-takers ADRF identified set under Lee-Liu sufficient-set selection by replacing pointwise Frechet pi_AT(X) plug-ins with a global denominator-coupled support-function program.
seed_details.referee_objection: A counterexample family is too small to matter and just restates the prior failed proposal warning.
seed_details.rebuttal: Promoting the obstruction to a strict regularity region would make the failure structural rather than anecdotal, but it is weaker than the full characterization seed.

seed_details.one_line: A kernel-localized doubly robust estimator of the Dinkelbach endpoint is asymptotically linear on a strict-margin regular submodel.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Develop valid endpoint estimation and inference for the Lee-Liu sufficient-set ADRF once its sharp endpoint is computed by a kernel-localized, thresholded, denominator-coupled program.
seed_details.referee_objection: Ben-Michael plus Colangelo-Lee already give this once the endpoint is written down.
seed_details.rebuttal: Neither paper covers a globally coupled ratio endpoint with an estimated active selector; the regular map still needs a new orthogonalization and root-map linearization.

seed_details.one_line: Uniform identified-set inference at active-set ties is governed by the directional derivative of the Dinkelbach root map, not an ordinary delta method.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Develop valid endpoint estimation and inference for the Lee-Liu sufficient-set ADRF once its sharp endpoint is computed by a kernel-localized, thresholded, denominator-coupled program.
seed_details.referee_objection: Too open-ended; without a concrete handle it is just a research wish.
seed_details.rebuttal: The handle is specific: derive the directional derivative of the transformed endpoint and pair it with Fang-Santos resampling; still better posed as an OEQ than as a conjectured theorem.

seed_details.one_line: A finite-bin piecewise-affine threshold formula approximates the continuous-dose sufficient-set endpoint and exposes the active constraints explicitly.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Compute an explicit active-constraint threshold for a finite adjacent-rank continuous-dose IV/copula LP, as a reusable certificate for denominator-coupled sharp bounds that are not pointwise separable.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a recycled finite LP strengthening path from a different proposal family.
seed_details.rebuttal: Useful as technique scaffolding, but it is not the sharpest angle for the present Lee-Liu target.

seed_details.one_line: Retained assignment memory changes the principal-stratum feasible polytope and yields a distinct linear-fractional always-taker/complier endpoint.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Derive sharp principal-stratum linear-fractional endpoint programs for always-taker/complier mixture deconvolution when retained assignment memory changes the feasible response-type polytope.
seed_details.referee_objection: Too far from the Lee-Liu sample-selection substrate to support this run's topic.
seed_details.rebuttal: The denominator obstacle is analogous, but the state space and consumer differ enough that it should stay a separate future pivot.

seed_details.one_line: On the strict-margin submodel, the sufficient-set endpoint has a semiparametric efficiency bound distinct from both point-identified ADRF and conditional-LP bounds.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Develop valid endpoint estimation and inference for the Lee-Liu sufficient-set ADRF once its sharp endpoint is computed by a kernel-localized, thresholded, denominator-coupled program.
seed_details.referee_objection: Efficiency is second-order packaging unless the identification map itself is already solved.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; as a standalone angle it is dominated by the identification kernel and is best treated as a later rung.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Lee (2009) is the baseline sample-selection result: once the always-taker mass is identified, sharp trimming yields binary-treatment Lee bounds.
Lee and Liu (2025) extend that logic to continuous or multivalued treatments under sufficient treatment values or sufficient sets; the sufficient-set covariate case leaves the always-taker share only Frechet bounded.
The unresolved issue is law-level rather than pointwise: the always-taker ADRF endpoint is a ratio with denominator E[pi_AT(X)], so endpoint-favorable selector choices at each X need not be jointly sharp.
Jonzon, Gabriel, Sjolander, and Sachs (2025) provide the exact conceptual warning by separating pointwise from uniform sharpness after covariate conditioning and averaging.
Semenova (2024) is the closest Lee-bound method template because it gives support-function characterization and orthogonal inference with covariates, but there the relevant always-taker law is identified instead of set-valued.
Dinkelbach (1967) gives the scalar transform that naturally fits a denominator-coupled endpoint problem and suggests a threshold characterization of the optimal selector.
Ben-Michael (2025) handles bounds that are expectations of conditional LP optima and debiases them, but the present problem is not an expectation of independent conditional optimizers; it is a globally coupled fractional program.
Colangelo and Lee (2025) provide the kernel-localized doubly robust score architecture for continuous-treatment ADRFs before the nonsmooth partial-ID step.
Fang and Santos (2019) are the right inference analogue once active-set ties make the endpoint map directionally differentiable rather than smooth.
In-repo warnings from pid_dose_response_shape_v1 show that pointwise continuous-dose fibers can fail to paste into law-level sharp bounds without an additional primitive, which is exactly the obstruction here.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / ass:interior-curvature
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The regular inference result is conditional on a novel smooth-root interior-curvature submodel and does not claim coverage at selector ties or Frechet-boundary optima.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Gaussian Wald inference is assessed only on laws satisfying ass:interior-curvature; boundary-uniform coverage remains the OEQ.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / ass:band-estimability and ass:trimmed-numerator-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The endpoint CLT is conditional on high-level L2 estimability of the Frechet-band endpoints and trimmed numerator process; those nuisance rates are not claimed to follow from Lee-Liu or Semenova.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The regular endpoint expansion is a conditional high-level nuisance-rate claim, not a theorem deriving first-stage rates from primitive smoothness classes.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / finite-bandwidth localization
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The estimator is centered through an explicit finite-bandwidth bias term and reaches the displayed Gaussian law only under local dose smoothness and undersmoothing.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The Wald claim is bound to the smooth finite-bandwidth regime where sup_lambda |B_{sigma,lambda,n}(P)|=O(h_n^2) and sqrt(n h_n) h_n^2 -> 0.

banked_reason: D0.5 revise-exhausted NO-PASS, but MATH IS SOUND field-tier (novelty referee passed): sharp covariate-conditional identified set for the always-takers ADRF as a support-function/Dinkelbach program + iff pointwise-plugin sharpness condition C + plug-in consistency estimator; the two real root gaps (measurable Frechet completion via Ionescu-Tulcea; ULLN density-lower-bound domination) were genuinely closed; parked at the D0.5 deep-construction-rigor boundary (residual measurability/domination sub-steps + dependency hygiene the strict referee keeps probing). Re-raisable: not a kernel failure.
